summary: Matched stage function grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 by changing its return type from void to f32, preserving the magnitude returned by lbVector_Normalize, returning it at function end, and updating the owning header declaration. Bot report showed one new match for main/melee/gr/grbigblueroute with grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 moving from 98.60% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Function definition updated: grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 now returns f32, stores lbVector_Normalize result in mag, and returns it after existing Vec3 clamping/zeroing logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 signature changed void -> f32; lbVector_Normalize(v) result assigned to f32 mag; return mag added
human_pr_text: function returns f32 magnitude from lbVector_Normalize

key_files.role: Owning header declaration updated to match recovered f32 return type.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: header declaration changed from void grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64(Vec3*) to f32 grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64(Vec3*)
human_pr_text: definition and header declaration updated accordingly

what_changed.text: Changed grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 return type from void to f32 in both the C definition and header prototype.
Captured lbVector_Normalize(v)'s returned magnitude in a local named mag instead of discarding it.
Returned mag after the existing post-normalization Vec3 component bounds checks and fallback zeroing logic.
The PR body claimed objdiff reports 100%, and the bot report recorded one new match: grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 from 98.60% to 100.00%, +4 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Recovered the true return value rather than treating lbVector_Normalize as a side-effect-only call, allowing the function to match without wider restructuring.
Updated the header prototype together with the function body, avoiding a stale or misleading declaration after signature recovery.
Kept the change narrowly scoped to the matched function and its declaration.

decomp_lessons.text: When a normalized vector helper returns a magnitude, check whether the caller's original function also returns that magnitude; a previously void decompilation may be discarding the value needed for a match.
After recovering a non-void return type in a function body, update the owning header declaration in the same focused change.

naming_conventions.text: The recovered lbVector_Normalize return value was named mag, a concise role name for magnitude.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use the callee return value directly as a local, then return it at the end, when matching evidence indicates the original function propagates a helper's return value.
Signature/prototype correction can be the matching tactic: here the key change was void -> f32 for grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64, with no data, pragma, or inline-assembly edits shown.

follow_up_queries: header prototype updated after function return type recovery

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body describes match, f32 magnitude return, header update, objdiff 100%
raw_diff: exact code and header changes
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists one new match for grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64
notes: No review comments were present in the loaded slice.
The objdiff 100% claim is supported by both PR body text and the loaded bot report; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The slice only shows this focused signature/body/header change; no broader stage behavior claims are inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate helper return values when signature recovery requires it
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2697 matched grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64 by changing the function from void to f32, storing the f32 magnitude returned by lbVector_Normalize(v), and returning that value after existing Vec3 adjustment logic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep header prototypes truthful after body recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: When grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64's recovered body proved a f32 return type, the PR updated src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.h from void to f32 in the same change.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that every lbVector_Normalize caller should return its magnitude; this PR only proves that behavior for grBigBlueRoute_8020DD64.
Do not infer reviewer intent or review guidance; the loaded slice contains zero review comments.
Do not promote the local variable name mag as a global naming rule; it is only a supported local naming example in this PR.
